string.text: Cannot find any active PDA registered under selected name
PDA signal lost
found with id
PDA Signal
Tracking active signal of a PDA registered under selected name.
Pay %s RUB?
Can't afford (%s / %s)
Private message:

Anonymous:
Looks like a new face finally found its way inside our Infopreneur Target Tracking System. Well, as long as you have enough money, you are always welcome to use it for your profits.
 
However, you should know: if you mess with our business,
we will use the same system to track you down.
 
You can't hide from us.
So stay at our good side and we will be able to help each other.
Anonymous:\nHey. You might be wondering who i am and how i managed to get into your closed private messages.\nThe answer is: none of your business.
Anonymous:\nI and my "colleagues" from the infopreneur network have been watching you for some time, and we find your methods... interesting.\nI have a proposal for you.
Anonymous:\nYour PDA has just been upgraded with one of our special tools, designed for tracking our targets. If you have enough money and a strong desire to find someone, all we need is their name.

Anonymous:
The catch? By running this program, you are automatically agreeing on giving some of your PDA's processing power towards assisting us in tracking other stalkers around you.
 
Some people might not enjoy having a snitch by their side.
Anonymous:\nOnce you are ready, just launch the new app on your PDA and enter your target's name.
Request denied: Target's name is missing.
Request denied: Target signal lost.
Huh, whoever that was, their PDA went offline right before you clicked "Accept."
Transferring %s RUB
Request denied: insufficient access level

This guy's tech got some advanced encryption protocols. We don't have enough agents with such devices inside the Zone to properly track him. If you upgrade your PDA, it might make finding the signal easier for us.
Sorry, bud, we can't serve you without a decent payment from your side. The servers and other equipment won't pay for themselves.
That's weird, this guy's PDA is registered under two different accounts. He might have stolen it from someone else.
A recently registered account. Are you planning to rob a novice?
That person's PDA did not even have any protection. Easy job.
That might be a new guy, but don't get too cocky. Even a rusty shotgun hurts like a bitch, if you load it with slugs.
Judging by his equipment, he might die even before you get close to him.
I don't think he has any armor, so you probably only need just a couple of shots.
His password is "12345". Do some people just never change built-in settings?
That guy forgot to change his location sharing privacy settings. You might not even need our help to keep track of him.
Low level encryption. Easy to crack.
Just another notch on your gun, isn't it?
Bonus points if you get him with a knife. Just kidding, don't be stupid.
Are you sure he's worth your effort?
This PDA must be some kind of an old model, there's no protection.
This guy keeps sending some kind of a signal to an unknown contact. Weird, but makes it easy to track him.
Cheap equipment, shouldn't be too difficult to deal with him.
Don't forget, most stalkers don't travel alone.
Another potential client about to be taken out. I guess he should've paid us first.
Remember, even though throwing bolts at an anomaly might discharge it and let you run through, it's always safer to go around. Most novice stalkers die from getting too cocky with their knowledge.
This guy knows a lot, but is too cocky for his own good. Keep testing his patience and he will make a mistake.
This PDA is registered under a different name, but its changes history shows a match with the name you entered. Stolen identity?
Huh, there are two different PDA's under this name. Another attempt at hiding from us?
Watch out for mutants. With all the stalker-on-stalker combat, these guys keep getting a lot more free space to breed.
I'd say "Don't tell anyone!", but most stalkers here already know about this system. They just value their lives too much to attempt messing with our business.
Another day, another pile of bodies.
That man has seen his fair share of battles. Make sure you don't become just another piece of his experience.
That one got really good at dealing with mutants, but we both know who's the real monster here.
Our database tells us this guy might've been an actual war veteran before entering the Zone. Stolen valor, or a real thing? Up to you to find out.
Remember to sell any PDA's you find to your local infopreneur, we highly value any information they might contain.
Feel free to dump an entire mag into his knees. That will teach him and his friends to pay us next time.
Don't forget to load your weapons.
That guy is known for his leadership skills. Better approach him while he's not around his squad.
This guy describes himself as an expert every time he posts something in his social media. Will you put that to the test?
That guy knows what he's doing, but you're not new here either, ain't that right?
Aim for the head.
Have fun.
Better watch your back, someone just paid us a ton of money for tracking you. Nothing personal.
This one's a tough target, are you sure you want to deal with him?
Strong encryption. That guy must be rich.
A tough nut to crack. Approach carefully.
Don't die, we need your money.
Better bring AP ammunition.
Don't engage him in close quarters.
That man sent us a ton of money to make sure we won't track him. Pay us double and we will reconsider his contract.
That's a well known stalker, he's killed over a couple hundred men. Better check your equipment before approaching him.
High tech armor and equipment. Don't fight that beast at close range.
Try not to kill all of my clients. It's not cheap to maintain our servers in these conditions.
Good luck, you will need it.
Remember, running away is always an option.
Another one of the new guys. Nothing worth mentioning yet.
Just a new stalker. The profile picture is just a photo of their cat. They have no clue what they've gotten themselves into.
I don't know that one, must be new here.
Making new friends, or..?
Got it. Tracking.
So, what's your plan? Assist them, or end their misery?
Just a random guy. Nobody's going to miss him.
That PDA is such an old model, it's surprising that it's still capable of sending any signal at all.
That guy owes a lot of money to Sid. Looks like that fat bastard got another person with his bullshit pricing.
Don't forget, if you can get your hands on their PDA's, we will pay a decent sum for any useful information stored in them.
Alright, hope finding him is worth your money.
Not sure why you would need to find him, but hey, if you are paying, we are tracking.
Just a guy, nothing special about him.
This man once claimed he's been at the Oasis, but he never provided any proof. If he happens to die, check his pockets for some information.
That man's got a long criminal record outside. No wonder he's in the Zone right now.
Remember to keep your movement smooth. The Zone takes those who rush.
Target acquired, activating movement trackers.
Money received, pleasure doing business with you.
"A professional stalker", says his profile. Too many of these "professionals" die nowadays.
Some kind of anomaly keeps distorting his signal, but our movement prediction system is prepared for that.
This man used to be one of our agents, but then decided it's too immoral to track people. I won't be too upset if you put a bullet in his head.
Make sure you bring enough meds for your work.
Good luck.
There he is. Check your map.
Got the signal, but no more info. You're on your own here.
That guy has a ton of money on his account, but his tech is of such a low quality. Is he trying to save up for his family, or something?
Last time i watched this one, a lot of friendly signals around him disappeared from the map. That guy left some people to die.
Watch your equipment, you don't want to be slowed down by useless trash during combat.
Found coordinates that supposedly lead to one of his stashes, but i'm not sharing that with you. Nothing personal, just business.
Detecting a lot of mutant activity in your area. Don't roam around without a shotgun.
Huh, that man's missing from our database. Where did he come from?
That man has a bounty on his head. Who did he mess with?
Location history shows this guy has been using some weird unknown routes to move around the Zone. We might need to update our maps.
If you're planning to take him out, better bring something to get through his armor.
There's a bit of interference, but it shouldn't be a problem.
Also, a couple of stalkers nearby reported seeing some kind of a.. flying dark cloud.. spitting boiling blood at people? That's some really weird shit.
The system says that guy's using a cellphone instead of a PDA. I thought those couldn't catch a proper signal in the Zone. Did he get some new model?
That man's location history is all over the place, like if he was trying to hide the last position he's been in.
This one has a ton of contracts with the mercs, what is he up to?
Man, there's some really weird shit in his private messages. I don't think i will be able to fall asleep today.
You might want to have some explosives on you. Never know when they could come in handy.

Hey, i know this system is mostly designed to help us kill people, but try to avoid committing unnecessary murder. Our statistics show that we've been losing more and more stalkers recently. If this keeps up, we might lose control of the Zone.
Apparently that guy is a well known stalker among his people. I wonder what he's done to deserve all of that attention.
There's a ton of interference around this guy's signal. Might be an electra or some kind of an artifact.
Woah, his location history shows sudden changes, like if he was instantly traveling very long distances. Has somebody learned how to use space anomalies for their benefit?
You're not planning to kill another potential client of ours, are you?
I hope you know what you're doing.
Whatever you are planning, good luck.
That person's pretty new. Also, for some reason they have your account in their friend list.
This one has set their location data as public info. Very naive.
That one's willingly showing their location to you. Are you friends or something?
You are just coming to help out a new guy, right?
Okay, let's see where this goes.
Let me guess, you're planning to relieve this one of their problems... whatever that means.
That one's new in the Zone. Seems to approve whatever you do.
This guy seems to trust you enough to let you view his location.
That one has been saying good things about you in some of his private messages.
You're not planning to rob a friendly new guy, are you?
Interesting to see you move to help out new stalkers. Or do you have different plans for this one?
Not sure what business you could have with a new guy, most can't even pay for an extra medkit.
The text on this guy's PDA doesn't look like any kind of encryption we know. Wait, did he just fill it with random letters?
Location history shows this guy has been traveling between multiple different hostile faction bases. Might be a contract worker or some kind of a spy.
Target acquired. Wait, no, that's the wrong one.. uhh... Target acquired!

Hey, i got reports of stalkers hearing some weird voices from their radios, so be careful when traveling around your area. It might be a powerful controller or some kind of a new mutant messing with our devices.
He doesn't seem hostile to you, so i suggest holding fire until you're sure you want him dead.
Request received, tracking the target.
This one's map shows some underground facility network under... huh... We will need to investigate that one.
This guy's online balance is -4294967295RUB. What the hell was he doing on his account?
This one apparently has an enormous artifact collection stored somewhere. It's a shame i can't find any more info on his PDA.
Hey, if he ever speaks about whatever he's messing with, you know who's always ready to buy this info from you.
If he ever happens to have an unfortunate accident and lose his PDA, we'd like to get our hands on that.
If you can avoid killing him, that would be nice for us. We'd like to question him at some point.
This guy's PDA has DOOM installed on it. Classic.
I've read this one survived getting shot in his torso about 30 times. Must've had some kind of a protective or a healing artifact.
Another one of those old-school stalkers. These guys never bother to use bolts or any sort of special tech to avoid danger because they learned to feel presence of anomalies and mutants on their own.
Don't forget about dangers of the Zone. Being around "friendly" people doesn't equal safety.
Planning to assist with their work, or to take their profits?
Before you commit any murder, try talking to some of these guys. Who knows what new information they might be holding.
Judging by his social media, he seems to be okay with your presence in the Zone. Could be a good companion.
That one's a real expert. Could be a good pick for a squad, unless somebody else snatched him already.
That one has over a hundred of different jobs listed on his PDA. Not sure how he's planning to fulfill all the promises he made.
You know the drill, just check your map.
Another day, another job.
By the way, i know it's none of my business, but why does your location history show that you were... nevermind.
That man has been in the Zone for a very long time. Looking at the amount of places he's been in, it's surprising he's still alive.
This one used to be a bounty hunter. Must've gotten enough profit to have some decent equipment.
That guy seems to actually know what he's doing. Might be good to have as an ally.
Payment accepted, placing markers on the map.
I'm getting really curious about what business you have with these people. Remember, if you get any useful information, you can always sell it to us.
Hey, i just got information that we once again lost contact with at least three of our agents at same time. It could be some kind of a well coordinated attack. If i were you, i would watch your back.
That guy's one of my best clients, it's going to cost you a bit more to make me consider giving away his position.
That one's an actual legend in these lands. Would make a powerful ally, if you can persuade him to work with you.
That one has achieved a lot in the short time he spent in the Zone. Might be useful to have him as your companion.
Going after legends, huh? This is getting interesting.
Think twice before trying to stab that one in the back.
I must say, you are interacting with some really interesting people these days.
Got it, waiting for the next target.
No refunds.
Hope you got what you paid for.
Signal lost. Was that your work?
He's gone, good job. Or bad job? Depends on what you were planning.
He's gone from our maps, you're on your own now.
Huh, i thought this guy died a long time ago. Why is his PDA still active?
Hey, this guy is listed as one of the stalkers that went missing a month ago! Perhaps you could go and ask him how he survived.
Oh, that's the guy that promised to deliver me some controller brain tissue! Could you go and ask if he managed to get it?
Wait a second... oh... i see... nevermind...
Hold on, actually, at this point you might be going after a group of zombies.
Hmm, weird, at some point his message history just turned into a bunch of gibberish... what happened?
Yeah, we have that guy in our database. He's a real gullible idio... *cough* i mean, a very experienced stalker, and our highly valued client!
Holy shit, that man's right on top of you, get out of there!.. Wait... it's just you.

Hey, what the hell are you doing? Why are you looking up your name? What are you trying to find out? All i can help you with is recovering your deleted browser history, we have all of that stored in our database.
I know we don't take payment for simply checking if a person exists, but it doesn't mean that we don't spend resources on that! Quit wasting our time and looking up random names!
Yeah, i'm familiar with that asshole. At this very moment he's wasting limited resources of our precious target tracking system just to look up his own fucking name!
This one exists in our database, but the PDA is either offline or deep underground. He might be dead or hiding.
I know that person, but can't get their signal. Perhaps someone else got to them before you?
We had his signal some time ago, but it might be too late to go after him now. I'll notify you if he gets back online.
That man exists, but is nowhere on our maps. I think the Zone might've taken this one.
Haven't seen any info about him in while. He might be dead.
Track Stalkers By Name
Required player rank to unlock the tracking tool

Player must reach this rank to use the tracking tool.
If you already have unlocked the tool in your save, changing this value does nothing.
1 = novice
2 = trainee(1999)
3 = experienced(3999)
4 = professional(6999)
5 = veteran(9999)
6 = expert(14999)
7 = master(20999)
8 = legend(27999)
Message display time(in seconds)
How long messages from infopreneur network stay on the screen before disappearing. Setting this to 0 disables these messages.
Infopreneur messages sound
Sound of messages that appear when you're using the tracking tool. Doesn't apply to messages that appear when you reach the correct rank for the first time.
Maximum trackable rank (PDA 1.0)
Maximum trackable rank (PDA 2.0)
Maximum trackable rank (PDA 3.0)

Ranks above selected can't be tracked by this PDA
1 = novice
2 = trainee(1999)
3 = experienced(3999)
4 = professional(6999)
5 = veteran(9999)
6 = expert(14999)
7 = master(20999)
8 = legend(27999)

Ranks above selected can't be tracked by this PDA
1 = novice
2 = trainee(1999)
3 = experienced(3999)
4 = professional(6999)
5 = veteran(9999)
6 = expert(14999)
7 = master(20999)
8 = legend(27999)

Ranks above selected can't be tracked by this PDA
1 = novice
2 = trainee(1999)
3 = experienced(3999)
4 = professional(6999)
5 = veteran(9999)
6 = expert(14999)
7 = master(20999)
8 = legend(27999)
Base cost of using the tool
Used to dynamically set final cost, depending on the target relation and rank multipliers set below
Friend cost multiplier
Neutral cost multiplier
Enemy cost multiplier
Base cost gets multiplied by this value depending on if the stalker is a friend/neutral/enemy
Base cost gets multiplied by this value depending on if the stalker is a friend/neutral/enemy
Base cost gets multiplied by this value depending on if the stalker is a friend/neutral/enemy
Novice cost multiplier
Trainee cost multiplier
Experienced cost multiplier
Professional cost multiplier
Veteran cost multiplier
Expert cost multiplier
Master cost multiplier
Legend cost multiplier
Base cost gets multiplied by this value depending on the stalker's rank
Base cost gets multiplied by this value depending on the stalker's rank
Base cost gets multiplied by this value depending on the stalker's rank
Base cost gets multiplied by this value depending on the stalker's rank
Base cost gets multiplied by this value depending on the stalker's rank
Base cost gets multiplied by this value depending on the stalker's rank
Base cost gets multiplied by this value depending on the stalker's rank
Base cost gets multiplied by this value depending on the stalker's rank
Enter the name:
Unknown frequency
No one's seen them recently. Snitch can't help.
Snitch is laying low. Try again in %d seconds.
somewhere in the Zone
Lost the trail. Your money's back.
He's at %s right now
Spotted near %s
Last seen at %s
He's in %s
heading north
heading north-east
heading east
heading south-east
heading south
heading south-west
heading west
heading north-west
holding position
This isn't personal... I just need the money.
Nothing personal.
Money's money. Don't ask again.
He's a bastard. I'd have done it for free.
Burn him.
Hope you make it hurt.
This never happened.
You didn't hear it from me.
Forget my voice.
Easy money.
Pleasure doing business.
Witness search radius (m)
How far around the target the snitch will look for someone who saw them. Larger = easier to find tips.
Base lie chance
Chance the snitch lies about location and direction.
Bandit/renegade lie chance
Chance the snitch lies when the witness is a bandit or renegade.
Per-name cooldown (sec)
How long to wait before asking the snitch about the same name again.
